title: Digital Connection vs. Mental Well-being
content: Title slide introducing a presentation on the relationship between digital connection and mental well-being. Presented by Li Wei for a Global Studies 201 course.
key_claims: Presentation topic: Digital Connection vs. Mental Well-being

title: The Ubiquitous Landscape
content: This slide describes Gen Z as digital natives who experience life primarily through an online lens. It highlights high daily usage rates of major social media platforms among students, emphasizing that constant connectivity has become an indispensable part of modern life.
key_claims: Gen Z are recognized as Digital Natives, experiencing life through an online lens
Constant connectivity is essentially indispensable in the modern era

title: Usage and Anxiety Statistics
content: This slide presents data linking heavy digital device usage to mental health outcomes, using a bar chart format. It shows high rates of daily usage exceeding two hours, alongside reported anxiety/sadness and increased depression risk.

title: Psychological Manifestations
content: This slide outlines three key psychological effects of social media use: FOMO, social comparison, and cyberbullying. Each is presented with an icon and brief description of its impact on mental well-being.
key_claims: FOMO Effect: fear of missing out creates significant stress and anxiety regarding social inclusion
Comparison: constant exposure to curated feeds leads to negative self-image and low self-esteem
Cyberbullying: nearly 87% of teens have experienced some form of online harassment or negative interactions

title: Strategic Mitigation Efforts
content: This slide presents three strategies to address the negative mental health effects of digital connectivity: digital detox, awareness, and platform responsibility. Each strategy is paired with an icon and brief explanation.
key_claims: Digital Detox: implementing time limits is a crucial strategy for emotional balance
Awareness: education regarding platform algorithms is essentially significant
Platform Responsibility: companies should prioritize user well-being as a primary imperative
